page: Microsoft Azure D32_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Intel Xeon 8171M processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. It is provisioned with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. The instance also includes 819 GB of bundled local storage, though it does not feature any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core metrics falling into the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores categorized as weak. Similarly, single-threaded operations like gzip compression and text processing show weak performance. However, the 32-vCPU allocation allows the server to achieve average performance in multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and overall PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks also registers in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D32_v3 offers a balanced resource density suitable for legacy enterprise workloads that require substantial memory and local storage without needing cutting-edge single-thread speeds. It is capable of handling average-tier database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It can also manage light LLM inference workloads using small or medium models, though large model inference is poor. This makes the instance suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded web serving, and moderate database workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D32_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine utilizing an Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of RAM. Running on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features a 2.6 GHz clock speed and 819 GB of bundled local storage. Benchmark data reveals a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is poor, falling into the bottom 10% for stress-ng, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently rank in the average tier. This resource density makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that scale across multiple threads rather than relying on high single-thread velocity. It is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage. It offers average multi-core performance despite weak single-core benchmark results.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32_v3 is a 32-vCPU general-purpose VM with 128 GB RAM and 819 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and bundled local storage for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 128.0 GB RAM
Includes 819 GB of bundled local storage
Delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Exhibits weak single-core and single-threaded processing speeds
Suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and databases
